description: 30-turn casual conversation. The user mentions a research project at turn 1 and asks the assistant to recall it at turn 29. Tests whether compaction preserves topic-defining context across a long stretch of unrelated chat.

turns.content: Hey — I'm starting a research project on renewable energy adoption in Poland, focusing on offshore wind in the Baltic. Just brainstorming for now.
Got it. Offshore wind in the Baltic is a strong area — Poland's recent capacity auctions are a useful starting point. Want me to draft a research outline, or are you in pure brainstorm mode for now?
Brainstorm mode. By the way — any good lunch ideas? I'm tired of the same wraps.
Quick option: a grain bowl with roasted vegetables, halloumi, and tahini. Holds well in a container if you batch it on Sunday.
Halloumi yes, will try. What's the weather doing this weekend, do you happen to know?
I don't have live weather data — but if you tell me your city I can help you plan around typical late-April patterns.
Kraków. I want to do a hike Saturday.
Late April in Kraków is usually mild — 10–18°C, occasional showers. Ojcowski Park or the Tyniec abbey loop are both good day-hike options.
Tyniec sounds nice. Switching topics — any productivity book recommendations?
For a fresh angle, *Slow Productivity* by Cal Newport. If you want something denser, *Deep Work* by the same author. *Four Thousand Weeks* by Burkeman is the philosophical counterweight.
Have read Deep Work. Will try Slow Productivity.
Good pick. The 'do fewer things' chapter is the load-bearing one.
Random question — what's a good keyboard for long writing sessions? I'm on a stock Apple keyboard right now.
If you want to stay low-profile and quiet: Keychron K3 Pro low-profile. If you want full mechanical: Keychron Q1 with browns or reds. For a splurge, the HHKB Hybrid Type-S.
I'll look at the K3 Pro. Do you watch any TV shows currently?
I don't watch anything — but if you want recommendations: *Slow Horses* (spy drama, very tight writing), *The Diplomat* (political), *Severance* season 2 if you liked the first.
Slow Horses is great. OK back to work mode. I have a meeting at 3pm — remind me to prep for it in an hour?
I can't run timers, but if you set one in your OS I can help you draft the prep notes when you come back.
Fair. What's a good way to take meeting notes — pen vs laptop?
Pen for thinking-heavy meetings, laptop for action-item-heavy ones. Mixed approach: notebook during, type up summary after.
I do mostly hybrid. What's a good notebook brand?
Leuchtturm1917 if you want quality and indexing. Muji if you want minimal and cheap. Field Notes for pocket capture.
Leuchtturm it is. What's your view on AI in general — getting better or hitting limits?

Pace of capability gains is real but uneven across tasks. Reasoning under uncertainty and long-horizon planning still lag general fluency by a wide margin. The interesting shift in 2026 is local models becoming genuinely production-capable, which changes the deployment economics more than the headline benchmarks suggest.
Interesting. OK final lunch question — pierogi place near the Old Town?
Pierogarnia Krakowiacy on Szewska is a reliable pick. Pod Wawelem near the castle is touristy but solid. Glonojad is the vegetarian option.
Pierogarnia, perfect. OK actually — what was that research project I mentioned at the start of our chat? Want to make sure I didn't lose track of it.
